briefingSummary.primaryRead: Three high-impact catalysts cluster early week — USD inflation + EUR policy tone dominate; gold and US indices most sensitive.
briefingSummary.operationalMeaning: Position size at 50% of normal risk for the catalyst window; widen exits ~30%; cancel marginal setups.
briefingSummary.currentRisk: 🟠🟠🟠🟠⚫ High — clustered catalyst exposure — reactivity elevated through the catalyst window; mean-reversion expected thereafter.

eventDayReference.expectedDuration: 60–120 minutes of high reactivity, then mean-reversion through next session
eventDayReference.whatToWatch: First 5-min close in the surprise direction; then 15-min close as the real trend signal. Cross-confirm against DXY and the lead pair (EURUSD).

higher.behaviour: USD bid, indices defensive, gold pressured intraday — first 30 min sees the cleanest directional move (event: CPI (USD))

traderAction.behavioralExplanation: After CPI (USD), only consider downside EURUSD continuation IF price fails to reclaim the pre-event reaction band around 1.0928. If EURUSD closes below 1.0928 and the next candle stays below it, the dollar-strength move is structurally real rather than a 60-second sweep.
confirmsContinuation: next 5-minute candle fails to recover back above 1.0928
DXY continues making higher highs through the same window
invalidatesContinuation: candle bodies start shrinking — momentum exhausting
DXY loses momentum immediately after the announcement spike
probableNextPath: price rotates toward the next liquidity zone beneath the event low (illustrative ~1.0880)
volatility stays elevated through the next macro catalyst window
probableFailurePath: event spike fades inside the first 10 minutes
EURUSD snaps back into the pre-event range
trapped short sellers forced to cover

higher.dollarImpact: Long EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 drawdown on $100k notional. Short EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 gain on $100k.

lower.behaviour: USD offered, indices bid, gold rallies on softening rate-path expectations — risk-on rotation (event: CPI (USD))

traderAction.behavioralExplanation: After CPI (USD), only consider upside EURUSD continuation IF price holds above the pre-event reaction band around 1.0928. If EURUSD closes above 1.0928 and the next candle stays above it, the dollar-weakness move is structurally real rather than a 60-second sweep.
confirmsContinuation: next 5-minute candle holds above 1.0928 without retracing back inside
DXY continues making lower lows through the same window
invalidatesContinuation: candle bodies start shrinking — momentum exhausting
DXY loses downside momentum immediately after the announcement spike
probableNextPath: price rotates toward the next liquidity zone above the event high (illustrative ~1.0980)
gold (XAUUSD) rallies in sympathy through the same window
probableFailurePath: dovish spike fades inside the first 10 minutes
EURUSD snaps back into the pre-event range
trapped long buyers forced to liquidate

lower.dollarImpact: Long EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 gain on $100k notional. Short XAUUSD: $500 – $1,500 drawdown on 100 oz notional exposure.

inline.behaviour: Light reaction across the board; initial spike either side then settle. Volatility drops within 15 minutes (event: CPI (USD))

traderAction.behavioralExplanation: An in-line print leaves the pre-event reaction band intact. Price will chop around 1.0928 but no side has structural control until a 15-min close breaks the band.
confirmsContinuation: both 5-min candles after the print stay between roughly 1.0915 and 1.0940
DXY mean-reverts back toward pre-event level inside 15 minutes
volatility (VIX-proxy) drops back to pre-event readings
invalidatesContinuation: a 5-min candle closes outside the 1.0915–1.0940 reaction band
DXY pushes to a fresh 15-min extreme in either direction
volume comes IN on a directional candle instead of fading
probableNextPath: range-bound chop through the next half-hour into the following catalyst
the real directional bias prints from the next catalyst, not from CPI (USD)
probableFailurePath: the in-line print masks a delayed reaction — the real direction prints 10–15 minutes after the announcement on a sweep through 1.0928
traders standing aside catch the move on the next ATLAS scan

inline.dollarImpact: Small swings $100 – $300 on $100k EURUSD, then mean-reversion back toward pre-event level.

reversal.behaviour: First move off the announcement is faded inside 10 minutes; the 15-min close is the real trend (event: CPI (USD))

traderAction.priceLevel: pre-event reaction band 1.0928 (15-min close direction is the real trend)
traderAction.behavioralExplanation: The first move off the announcement is being faded by larger volume on the other side. Trade only the post-15-min direction IF the 15-min candle closes through 1.0928 in the OPPOSITE direction of the initial spike.
confirmsContinuation: 15-min candle closes opposite to the first 5-min move
volume IN-flow visible against the initial direction
DXY mirrors the reversal on the same 15-min close
invalidatesContinuation: the 15-min close confirms the initial direction (no reversal)
candle body of the 15-min close is small relative to its wick
DXY continues in the initial-spike direction without retracing
probableNextPath: price extends in the post-15-min direction toward the opposite-side liquidity zone (~1.0880 for a hawkish-reversal, ~1.0980 for a dovish-reversal)
volatility stays elevated through the next macro catalyst window
probableFailurePath: the reversal candle itself gets faded inside the next 15 minutes
price returns to the pre-event range
trapped reversal traders forced to exit on the next ATLAS scan

reversal.dollarImpact: Traders who chase the first 60 seconds: $800 – $1,500 drawdown on $100k EURUSD before the reversal completes.

marketImpact.mechanism: cause: CPI (USD) → expectation: front-end rate-path repricing → market reaction: USD lead, cross-asset cascade through DXY, EURUSD, XAUUSD, US500
marketImpact.priceReactionPath: Initial impulse (0–60s) → first 5-min close confirms direction → 15-min close gives the real trend → 1H close locks in the bias for the rest of the session.
marketImpact.liquidityEffect: Spreads widen 2–4× across the announcement candle; market-depth thins through T+5; normalises by T+15.
marketImpact.volatilityEffect: ATR doubles on the announcement candle; expect overshoot beyond historical 1-σ
marketImpact.traderConsequence: Chasing the first 60 seconds creates the $800–$1,500 drawdown band; waiting for the 5-min close and trading the next pullback flips the same setup into a clean $300–$800 gain on $100k EURUSD.

riskEscalation.healthy: PRE-EVENT (T-60 → T-15): normal flow, positions sized to current mood. Optional pre-position only if structure already validated by Dark Horse scan.
riskEscalation.caution: T-15 → T-0: tighten stops on existing exposure; do not add new direction trades. Sit on hands for the announcement.
riskEscalation.danger: T+0 → T+5: first-candle spike. Do not enter inside this window — chase risk is at session peak. Watch only.
riskEscalation.invalidation: STAND-ASIDE: if the first 5-min close is opposite to your existing position direction, exit immediately at next 1-min close. RE-ENTRY: only after a confirmed 15-min close in the structural direction following CPI (USD).

whatToDoNow.action: Reduce dollar exposure to 50% of normal risk for the next 6 hours.
whatToDoNow.why: Catalyst-driven sessions widen spreads and stop ranges. Smaller dollar exposure keeps planned-risk discipline through the volatility expansion.
whatToDoNow.ifIgnored: A normal-size position takes ~30–50% more drawdown through the announcement candle than the planned-risk model assumes — turning a designed $500 loss into a $650–$750 actual loss.
whatToDoNow.confirmation: Position-size calculator reflects the reduced dollar exposure before the event window opens. If normal risk per trade is $500 on a $10k account, the new ceiling is ~$250.
whatToDoNow.actionChangesWhen: Market mood drops from HIGH back to LOW within the 6-hour window — at that point dollar exposure can scale back up gradually, ONE position at a time.
whatToDoNow.dollarConsequence: Reducing to ~$250 per trade caps the announcement-window drawdown at the planned-risk line; oversized exposure regularly turns a $500 designed loss into $700–$1,000 of realised damage.

whatToDoNow.action: Cancel marginal setups. Only act on triggers where the next candle closes BEYOND the level (confirmed directional structure test).
whatToDoNow.why: Sweeps and false breaks dominate the first 15 minutes after a high-impact print; trading on a wick alone is paying for false direction.
whatToDoNow.ifIgnored: Acting on the first 60 seconds typically catches the wick high or low; the $300–$800 drawdown band on $100k EURUSD is paid for the privilege.
whatToDoNow.confirmation: No new entries until the 5-minute candle CLOSES beyond the structural level AND the next candle holds the level as new support/resistance.
whatToDoNow.actionChangesWhen: Two consecutive 5-min closes confirm direction AND the lead pair shows momentum agreement on the trigger timeframe.
whatToDoNow.dollarConsequence: Avoids the $300–$800 drawdown band typical of chasing a first-move fake; the same setup post-confirmation pays $300–$800 in agreement direction.

whatToDoNow.action: Widen exit-points by ~30% on existing positions; tight exits get hit before direction confirms.
whatToDoNow.why: Initial volatility expansion pushes price beyond historical 1-σ before the real direction prints — tight exits convert noise into realised loss.
whatToDoNow.ifIgnored: Tight exits get swept by the announcement-candle wick; the position is closed at a loss right before the structural direction confirms — the worst possible exit point.
whatToDoNow.confirmation: All open exits sit at least 1.3× the pre-event ATR distance away from current price by 5 minutes before the announcement.
whatToDoNow.actionChangesWhen: The 15-min candle close prints the real direction; tighter exits can be reapplied 30 minutes after confirmation if the move is mean-reverting.
whatToDoNow.dollarConsequence: Avoids being stopped on a wick that mean-reverts within 5 minutes — protects $150–$400 of carry on $100k notional through the event window.

confirmationCancellation.confirmsWhen: First 5-min close in the surprise direction PLUS confirming structure on DXY / lead pair.
confirmationCancellation.cancelsWhen: First 15-min close fails to follow through OR geopolitical override hits the wire inside the window.
confirmationCancellation.dangerIf: Position is opposite the 5-min close direction by T+5 — exit immediately at next 1-min close. No averaging into a fresh catalyst-led move.

todaysAnnouncements.title: CPI (USD)
todaysAnnouncements.whyItMatters: Inflation print directly repricing the rate-path. Surprise direction drives DXY and front-end yields, cascading through every dollar pair, gold, and US indices.

todaysAnnouncements.title: Non Farm Payrolls
todaysAnnouncements.whyItMatters: Labour print sets the Fed reaction function. Surprise direction repricing rate-path expectations through DXY and bonds; gold and indices follow.

todaysAnnouncements.title: ECB Rate Decision
todaysAnnouncements.whyItMatters: Central-bank statement directly setting policy expectations. Forward guidance dominates the post-statement price action — the 15-min close after the press conference is the real trend.

primaryEventFocus.volatilityWindow: 60–120 minutes of high reactivity, then mean-reversion through next session
likelyPaths: HIGHER surprise → USD bid, EURUSD breaks 1.0928 lower, XAUUSD pressured, US500 defensive.
LOWER surprise → USD offered, EURUSD breaks 1.0928 upper, XAUUSD rallies, US500 bid.
IN-LINE print → reaction band intact, mean-reversion inside the next 15 min.
INITIAL-DIRECTION REVERSAL → first 60-second move faded; 15-min close prints the real trend.
primaryEventFocus.confirmation: First 5-min close + DXY / lead pair confirmation in agreement.
primaryEventFocus.cancellation: First 15-min close fails to follow through; geopolitical override hits the wire.

next24To72Hours.title: CPI (USD)
next24To72Hours.expectedSensitivity: Lead catalyst this cycle — directional gate for the next 6–24 hours.
next24To72Hours.preparationGuidance: Position size at 60% of normal in the 6 hours leading in; widen exits ~30% inside the announcement candle.

affectedMarketsExpanded.howAffected: Dollar-index basket; DIRECT primary indicator of USD repricing — leads every dollar pair.
affectedMarketsExpanded.strongerResult: DXY pushes to fresh 24-hour high on hawkish USD surprise; pairs follow.
affectedMarketsExpanded.weakerResult: DXY drops below pre-event range on dovish print; pairs rally in sympathy.
affectedMarketsExpanded.invalidation: 15-min close back inside the pre-event range; whipsaw without follow-through.
affectedMarketsExpanded.keyPriceLevels: Pre-event range top/bottom is the directional gate. Above range = dollar real, below = dollar fade.
affectedMarketsExpanded.riskNote: Leads every dollar pair by 5–10 minutes — read DXY first, then act on the pair.

affectedMarketsExpanded.howAffected: Dollar leg of the EUR-USD pair; reacts to USD rate-path repricing first, EUR fundamentals second.
affectedMarketsExpanded.strongerResult: EURUSD drops on stronger USD print — first 30 min sees the cleanest move.
affectedMarketsExpanded.weakerResult: EURUSD rallies on softer USD print — EUR-side optimism amplifies the move.
affectedMarketsExpanded.confirmation: 5-min candle close in the surprise direction PLUS DXY making higher highs / lower lows in agreement.
affectedMarketsExpanded.invalidation: Body close back through the pre-event reaction band (≈1.0928) within 15 minutes.
affectedMarketsExpanded.keyPriceLevels: Reaction band 1.0928 · downside liquidity ≈1.0880 · upside liquidity ≈1.0980 · 30-day ATR ≈70 pips.
affectedMarketsExpanded.riskNote: $300–$800 swing on $100k notional in the first 30 minutes; up to $1,500 on a reversal chase.

affectedMarketsExpanded.howAffected: Inverse to real-yields and USD; reacts secondary to USD prints, primary to risk-off flow.
affectedMarketsExpanded.strongerResult: XAUUSD drops intraday on a hawkish USD surprise; safe-haven flow can recover the move on a 1H close.
affectedMarketsExpanded.weakerResult: XAUUSD rallies on a dovish USD print; real-yield-fall amplifies.
affectedMarketsExpanded.confirmation: DXY direction in agreement + 1H close beyond the structural level.
affectedMarketsExpanded.invalidation: Body close back through the pre-event range; safe-haven flow flipping the move on the 1H.
affectedMarketsExpanded.riskNote: $500–$1,500 swing on 100 oz exposure in the first 30 minutes.

affectedMarketsExpanded.howAffected: Equity index repricing rate-path expectations; reacts to bond yields first, dollar second.
affectedMarketsExpanded.strongerResult: US500 drops on rate-hike fears after a hawkish USD print.
affectedMarketsExpanded.weakerResult: US500 rallies on rate-cut optimism after a dovish USD print.
affectedMarketsExpanded.confirmation: 5-min close + VIX direction (inverse) + yields in agreement.
affectedMarketsExpanded.invalidation: Body close back into the pre-event range OR VIX collapsing without index direction following.
affectedMarketsExpanded.keyPriceLevels: VWAP-driven; first 15-min VWAP becomes the intraday pivot.
affectedMarketsExpanded.riskNote: $400–$900 swing on 100-share exposure in the first 30 minutes.

priceMap.whyMatters: Post-event reaction band. ATLAS treats EURUSD direction as structurally real ONLY if price holds outside this band on the 5-min close.
priceMap.ifHolds: Continuation in the breakout direction toward the adjacent liquidity zone (1.0880 downside or 1.0980 upside).
priceMap.ifFails: Event fade — EURUSD returns into the pre-event range; trapped traders forced to cover.
priceMap.confirmation: 5-min close beyond the band + next candle stays beyond.
priceMap.invalidation: Body close back inside the band on the next candle.
priceMap.dollarConsequence: Failed continuation can reverse $300–$800 per $100k EURUSD notional in the first 15 minutes after invalidation.

priceMap.whyMatters: Downside liquidity below the event low; absorbs continuation flow on a confirmed hawkish print.
priceMap.ifHolds: Downside extension exhausts here; mean-reversion attempt toward 1.0928.
priceMap.ifFails: Deeper continuation toward 1.0850; volatility stays elevated through the next macro window.
priceMap.confirmation: 1H close below 1.0880 + DXY making higher highs.

priceMap.whyMatters: Upside liquidity above the event high; absorbs continuation flow on a confirmed dovish print.
priceMap.ifHolds: Upside extension exhausts here; mean-reversion attempt back toward 1.0928.
priceMap.ifFails: Deeper continuation toward 1.1010; risk-on flow stays in equities and gold.
priceMap.confirmation: 1H close above 1.0980 + DXY making lower lows.

priceMap.whyMatters: Dollar-index gate. DXY closing through the pre-event range tells ATLAS the dollar move is real BEFORE any individual pair confirms.
priceMap.ifHolds: Range respected → no directional dollar bias; pairs chop until the next catalyst.
priceMap.ifFails: Range broken → dollar lead in agreement direction; every dollar pair confirms within 5–10 minutes.
priceMap.confirmation: 15-min close beyond the range on broad-based dollar flow.
priceMap.invalidation: 15-min close back inside the range within 30 minutes.
priceMap.dollarConsequence: Reading DXY first protects $200–$500 per $100k notional vs. reading the pair direction in isolation.

priceMap.whyMatters: Structural support beneath current price. Gold below 2380 on a 1H close flips the macro bias bearish on the metal complex.
priceMap.ifHolds: Mean-reversion back toward 2410; safe-haven flow returns if equity risk-off prints.
priceMap.ifFails: Deeper liquidation toward 2350; correlation with real-yields tightens.
priceMap.dollarConsequence: Holding 100 oz exposure through a 2380 break: $300–$500 of unrealised loss before invalidation triggers.

priceMap.whyMatters: Structural resistance above current price. Gold above 2440 on a 1H close flips macro bias bullish on the metal complex.
priceMap.ifFails: Breakout toward fresh highs; real-yields fall, dollar weakness compounds.
priceMap.dollarConsequence: Riding 100 oz exposure through 2440: each $10 move = $1,000 of P/L.

operationalNarrative.whatTheMarketIsDoing: Liquidity thinning; pre-event positioning visible in front-end yields and DXY; equity desks de-grossing before the announcement candle.
operationalNarrative.whyItIsDoingIt: Market participants reprice the rate-path BEFORE the catalyst lands; once the announcement prints, the move is the difference between consensus and actual.
operationalNarrative.whatChangesNext: At T-0 the announcement prints; the 5-min close at T+5 is the FIRST real read on direction; the 15-min close at T+15 is the REAL trend; the 1H close locks in the session bias.
operationalNarrative.whatTradersShouldAvoid: New entries inside the announcement candle, adding to losers in the pre-event window, chasing the first 60-second move, oversizing into thin liquidity.
operationalNarrative.whenConditionsBecomeSaferAgain: After the 15-min close at T+15 confirms direction AND the 1H close at T+60 locks in the trend. Position-sizing can return to normal ~6 hours after the catalyst if no follow-on event is on the calendar.
